title: Crisp Citrusy Greek Beans Fasolada
description: A tangy and crisp Greek dish featuring poppy seeds, perfect for a brunch mood, this pescatarian-friendly fasolada is a delicious twist on traditional Greek cuisine. It combines the freshness of citrus with the comfort of beans.
instructions: Rinse the navy beans and soak them in water for at least 8 hours or overnight.
Drain and rinse the soaked beans, then place them in a pressure cooker with 4 cups of water.
Add the chopped onion, minced garlic, salt, and black pepper to the pressure cooker.
Close the lid and cook on high pressure for 20 minutes, then let the pressure release naturally.
In a small bowl, whisk together the lemon juice and olive oil.
Stir the lemon juice mixture into the cooked beans.
Sprinkle the poppy seeds over the top of the beans and serve.
Garnish with additional lemon wedges if desired.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or dill if desired.
